searchLedger.hypothesis: Simultaneous first acquisition without an existing owner lease is guarded

searchActions.result: The no-existing-lease path uses fs.openSync(..., 'wx') for exclusive creation (.opencode/bin/mk-skill-advisor-launcher.cjs:339-346); losers read the holder after EEXIST (.opencode/bin/mk-skill-advisor-launcher.cjs:431-438). The launcher also rechecks owner lease ownership before writing the PID guard and before spawning (.opencode/bin/mk-skill-advisor-launcher.cjs:978-982, 866-870).

searchLedger.rationale: Two launchers starting from no owner lease cannot both atomically create .skill-advisor-owner.json. A loser either bridges/reports or, if ownership changes before spawn, launchServer refuses to spawn.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: O_CREAT|O_EXCL-style acquisition plus owner reprobes prevent the no-prior-lease double-writer interleaving.

searchLedger.hypothesis: Partial-read framing and output serialization are shared with the reference path

searchActions.result: createFrameSplitter buffers until newline and uses StringDecoder for split UTF-8 at lines 74-100; writes serialize one JSON frame plus newline at lines 227-229, and output/socket queues serialize forwarding at lines 457-559. skill-advisor uses the same createSessionProxy path as spec-memory, differing only by classifier.

searchLedger.rationale: No skill-advisor-specific truncation/interleaving defect found in the message framing path. Partial reads, multibyte boundaries, and request/response tracking are handled in the shared proxy rather than reimplemented in the skill-advisor launcher.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: Proven-equivalent shared implementation to mk-spec-memory for framing; no divergent skill-advisor bridge code on this path.

searchLedger.hypothesis: Non-owner exit does not wipe another live launcher's owner lease

searchActions.result: clearOwnerLeaseFile no-ops unless local ownerLeasePid is set and the current lease ownerPid still matches that local pid at 500-507. clearLeaseFile only removes the PID lease when lease.pid matches this launcher or this launcher's child at 610-615. Secondary bridge-only launchers do not set ownerLeasePid or childProcess.

searchLedger.rationale: The prior bug class was an exit handler deleting a lease still owned by another live launcher. I do not see that specific interleaving in this target.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: Lease release is conditional on local ownership for the owner lease and on local pid/current child for the PID lease. The remaining lease-deletion risk is the missing release/adoption path covered by the P0 findings, not wiping another live owner's lease.

searchLedger.hypothesis: A single missed live-owner probe is not enough for this file to reap a healthy daemon

searchActions.result: The target delegates live-owner bridge classification to maybeBridgeLeaseHolder at 537-546 and has no direct single-probe reap path in checkStrictSingleWriter.

searchLedger.rationale: The target does not itself reap on the first missed probe. The concrete defect is the opposite: after the delegated bridge reports a respawn decision, the target ignores it.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: No target-side one-probe healthy-daemon reap path was found.

searchLedger.hypothesis: Production socket/database contact is isolated in the launcher orphan-reaping tests

searchActions.result: createWorkspace builds temp db/socket directories at lines 128-131, copies launcher/proxy fixtures into the temp workspace at lines 123-127, and spawnLauncher pins MK_SKILL_ADVISOR_DB_DIR plus SPECKIT_IPC_SOCKET_DIR to those temp paths at lines 150-159.

searchLedger.rationale: The tests do not bind the production socket path or use the production skill-advisor database for the reviewed launcher scenarios.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: Sandbox-critical env vars and fixture paths are temp-scoped for the reviewed tests.

searchLedger.hypothesis: Second-client bridge test does assert end-to-end message flow

searchActions.result: The test writes an initialize request to the second launcher's stdin at lines 316-326, waits for stdout close at line 328, and asserts stdout contains the fixture daemon serverInfo name warm-advisor at line 334.

searchLedger.rationale: This is not merely socket-open coverage; it proves a client frame traverses the lease-holder session proxy and a daemon response reaches stdout.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: The asserted stdout response is end-to-end evidence for this specific test.
